name: FreshBooks
description: Cloud accounting built for freelancers and service businesses
summary: If you are a freelancer or consultant who wants beautiful invoices and dead-simple time tracking without learning accounting, FreshBooks is your best bet.

name: QuickBooks
description: The industry standard for small business accounting and invoicing
summary: If you need serious accounting alongside invoicing — payroll, inventory, tax prep — QuickBooks is the safe choice. It does everything, even if the learning curve is real.

name: Zoho Invoice
description: Feature-rich free invoicing within the Zoho ecosystem
summary: If you already use Zoho products or want genuinely free invoicing with automation features, Zoho Invoice punches way above its price tag (which is zero).
pros: Completely free for up to 1000 invoices/year
cons: UI feels dated compared to FreshBooks

name: Wave
description: 100% free accounting and invoicing for small businesses
summary: If you are bootstrapping and need professional invoicing without spending a dollar, Wave is the real deal — free forever, no catch.

name: Square Invoices
description: Free invoicing that connects seamlessly to Square POS
summary: If you sell in-person AND online and want one system for everything — POS, invoicing, payments — Square ties it all together.

name: Invoice Ninja
description: Open-source invoicing with a self-hosting option
summary: If you want full control over your invoicing data with the option to self-host, Invoice Ninja is the developer-friendly choice.
